title: dactylorhiza elata subsp. ambigua
intro: Dactylorhiza elata subsp. ambigua is a rare subspecies of orchid, commonly known as the tall marsh-orchid or white marsh-orchid, which is native to certain parts of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. ambigua include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections, making it a valuable herb for those suffering from respiratory conditions. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza elata subsp. ambigua include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
